concepts.name: The Cellar Vigil
concepts.premise: A single long vertical descent through near-black and deep amber darkness, where one bottle photograph appears once, held in stillness under a single warm light while short lines of copy surface only on scroll, each section separated by pure negative space…

anchor.why: Generous 140-180px sections and unhurried whitespace are what let the page feel like standing quietly in a cellar rather than browsing a product.

concepts.name: One Agave, One Story
concepts.premise: A narrative magazine scroll with asymmetric columns, a duotone field-to-still photo essay of the palenque, agave, and hands, pull-quotes from the third-generation maker hanging into the margin, reading like a printed brand monograph rather than a sales page.

anchor.why: Multi-column text, pull-quotes, and duotone imagery give the family story the long-form editorial gravity the brief asks for.

concepts.name: The Single Pour
concepts.premise: An almost empty page where everything is quiet except one moment of deliberate contrast — the bottle rendered in full saturated color against an otherwise monochrome, low-contrast page — so the eye is pulled to that one image and nowhere else, with all other…

anchor.why: Isolating the bottle as the single differing element makes it the one thing remembered, reinforcing 'one bottle, one agave' literally in the visual hierarchy.
